source_pr.title: load data: format 'delimited data', defined null by
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref #40499

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?
- move default value of `FIELDS`/`LINES` clause from parser to planner
- add format 'delimited data'
- change `NULL DEFINED BY` to `FIELDS defined null by`

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: No concrete flaky failure signature is captured in this case. The PR is largely a feature/refactor around LOAD DATA parsing/planning ("delimited data", `FIELDS ... DEFINED NULL BY`, moving defaults from parser to planner); it briefly added a debug log for an "unstable case" and then reverted it.
failure_signature: No concrete failure signature (feature PR; only temporary debug logging for an unstable case, later reverted)
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR is primarily a large feature/refactor for LOAD DATA. The only "flaky"-looking signal in the patch is a commit labeled "add log to check a unstable case", which is immediately reverted in the next commit and does not constitute a stabilization fix or a root-cause explanation. Without the actual flaky failure signature (CI logs/stack/repro) that motivated the temporary log, we should not guess a root cause (certainly not result-order related). Keep this as insufficient evidence until concrete failure evidence is linked.
fix_pattern: Feature/refactor PR with only transient debug logging and no stable, targeted flaky fix; keep as insufficient evidence until CI logs/issues provide the failure signature and root cause.

evidence: evidence: add log to check a unstable case
